title: ExcelJS: “Uncaught EvalError: 'unsafe-eval' is not an allowed source of script in the following Content” in Angular App
author: pratik75
body:
I have deployed an Angular Application that uses ExcelJS library on IIS server. My current security policy forces me to return below header in IIS Http Response

content-security-policy: script-src 'self';img-src 'self'

With this setting, angular app is not loading and giving following error.

exceljs.js:87162 Uncaught EvalError: Refused to evaluate a string as JavaScript because 'unsafe-eval' is not an allowed source of script in the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self'".

Is there any way to override Http Response security Policy. I tried to add content security policy using But it did not work.

Could anyone please suggest how to run ExcelJS with strict content security policy?

comments.author: Noppey
comments.body:
Use the bare version by mapping it's path within tsconfig:
```
"exceljs": [
        "node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.bare.min.js"
]
```

In my situation, this resulted in another error, which was solved by adding:
`import 'regenerator-runtime/runtime';`

comments.author: thomas-lucas1985
Noppey
thomas-lucas1985
comments.body:
We have an import sorting rule in place, which places the import after the Workbook import. Could be the reason.
But I've managed to solve it on my side by adding 
`import 'regenerator-runtime/runtime'; `
in my polyfill file instead and this seems to solve all my issues.

Thanks, because without your post, I wouldn't have managed to solve it this quickly.

comments.author: achimmihca
comments.body:
Duplicate of #512
Duplicate of #713

I still find `Function('return this')()` in current versions of this lib, when looking in `node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.bare.min.js`.
Has no fix arrived yet in exceljs?

Why not just use `this` instead, or maybe one can use the `window` object instead, or is it maybe just an outdated dependency? Why use `Function('return this')()` in the first place?
